mechanism_narrative: A falling FEDFUNDS rate lowers the equity discount rate and supports risk appetite, so downward month-over-month changes in the policy rate predict positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Reject if 20-day forward SPX returns are not positive on average when FEDFUNDS is falling, or if triggered periods show Rank IC below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A CPIAUCSL acceleration above recent trend raises inflation surprise risk, increases expected policy restraint, and compresses equity multiples, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: CPIAUCSL 3-month annualized change rises above its prior 12-month median
failure_condition: Reject if inflation-acceleration episodes do not precede below-median 20-day SPX returns, or if the sign of the effect is not negative in at least 60% of triggered instances.

mechanism_narrative: A rising UNRATE indicates labor-market deterioration and higher recession probability, so a sustained increase in unemployment predicts negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE rises by at least 0.3 percentage points over 3 months
failure_condition: Reject if unemployment-rise episodes are followed by positive 60-day SPX returns more often than negative ones, or if the 60-day hit rate for negative returns falls below 55%.

mechanism_narrative: When PAYEMS stops rising while UNRATE is elevated, payroll stagnation confirms weak income growth and weak demand, which should pressure equity returns over the next 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: PAYEMS 3-month change is negative while UNRATE is at or above 5%
failure_condition: Reject if the joint condition does not produce lower 20-day SPX returns than either PAYEMS weakness or UNRATE elevation alone, or if the interaction term is not statistically distinguishable from zero.

mechanism_narrative: A sustained rise in RSAFS signals stronger nominal consumer spending and revenue growth, which should support S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Reject if positive RSAFS growth is not associated with above-baseline 20-day SPX returns, or if the signal loses predictive power in any 3-year rolling window with Rank IC below 0.03.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp decline in INDPRO signals production shutdowns and margin pressure, so weak industrial production growth should predict negative S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: Reject if negative INDPRO prints fail to precede weak 5-day SPX returns, or if the median 5-day return after negative INDPRO changes is not below zero.

mechanism_narrative: When HOUST rises while UNRATE falls, the combination signals a housing-led demand recovery and improving household balance sheets, which should lift S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
candidate_trigger: HOUST 3-month change is positive and UNRATE 3-month change is negative
failure_condition: Reject if the joint housing-labor recovery condition does not outperform either proxy alone for 60-day SPX returns, or if the conditional hit rate is not at least 55% positive.

mechanism_narrative: In a zero-rate environment, stable CPIAUCSL combined with a near-zero FEDFUNDS rate supports financial conditions and reduces discount-rate pressure, which should produce positive S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
candidate_trigger: FEDFUNDS below 0.25% and CPIAUCSL inflation not accelerating
failure_condition: Reject if low-rate, stable-inflation episodes do not yield positive 5-day SPX returns more often than not, or if the interaction effect becomes negative in rolling out-of-sample tests.
